title: Epacris micranthera
taxonomy: Epacris micranthera is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. This plant is native to Australia and is commonly known as the small-flowered red bells or the small-flowered scarlet bells. It is a member of the genus Epacris, which is composed of small to medium-sized shrubs found in eastern Australia and New Zealand. The species Epacris micranthera is further classified within the genus, which itself is classified within the family Ericaceae. The Ericaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the heath or heather family.

intro: Epacris micranthera, commonly known as the New Zealand heath, is a small shrub native to New Zealand, characterized by its dense, compact growth habit and small white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, potentially aiding in wound healing, fever reduction, and immune system support.
